title: Add new Study module: UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide (educational, no simulations)

requirements.text: Add a new Study module under "Tools & Instruments" titled "UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide" that is accessible from the Study home screen, without modifying other existing Study modules.

quotes:
Add a new Study module under "Tools & Instruments" titled:

"UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide"

acceptanceCriteria: A new navigation option/module card labeled "UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide" is visible on the Study home screen.
Selecting the new module opens a dedicated module view for the guide.
No text/content or behavior changes occur in other Study modules (including the existing "Multimeter Training" module).

requirements.text: Implement the "UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide" as structured, cleanly formatted educational content with distinct sections (headers and spacing) for each of the following functions: AC Voltage (VAC), DC Voltage (VDC), AC Amps (Clamp meter use), DC Amps (Inverter systems), Resistance (Ohms), Continuity, Capacitance (microfarads), Temperature measurement (dual thermocouples), Frequency (Hz), and Differential temperature (Delta T).

quotes:
Include structured educational content covering:

- AC Voltage (VAC) use in HVAC
- DC Voltage (VDC) use in modern systems
- AC Amps (Clamp meter use)
- DC Amps (Inverter systems)
- Resistance (Ohms) testing
- Continuity testing
- Capacitance (microfarads) testing
- Temperature measurement (dual thermocouples)
- Frequency (Hz)
- Differential temperature (Delta T)

Format as clean sections with headers and spacing.

acceptanceCriteria: The module view renders 10 clearly separated sections matching the listed functions.
Each section is readable and consistently formatted using clear headers and spacing (e.g., cards/separators consistent with existing Study modules).
All user-facing text in the module is in English.

requirements.text: For each function section in the "UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide", include the following subsections: When to use it, Power ON or Power OFF rules, HVAC-specific examples, Common mistakes, and Safety warnings.

quotes:
For each function include:
- When to use it
- Power ON or Power OFF rules
- HVAC-specific examples
- Common mistakes
- Safety warnings

acceptanceCriteria: Every one of the 10 function sections includes all five required subsections.
Power ON/OFF rules are explicitly stated for each function (not implied).
HVAC-specific examples are present in each section and relate to real HVAC diagnostic/measurement contexts.
Common mistakes and safety warnings are present in each section and are clearly distinguishable from the other subsections.

requirements.text: Ensure the new "UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide" module is educational and stable: do not add simulations or interactive tool behavior as part of this module.

quotes:
Do not add simulations.
Keep it educational and stable.

acceptanceCriteria: The new module contains educational content only (reading/scrolling/collapsing is acceptable), with no simulator widgets, dynamic measurement generators, or interactive tool simulations.
No existing simulator components under study tools are reused/embedded for this guide.

constraints: Do not modify other study modules’ content or behavior.
Do not add simulations to the new UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide module.
Respect frontend immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or identity/actor hooks.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Adding scenario-based practice questions for the UEi DL589 Multimeter Guide (not requested).
Creating or modifying any multimeter simulation tools or interactive simulators.
Backend changes (no new Motoko state, APIs, or migrations) unless required for basic navigation/content rendering.
